name: Appenzell Ausserrhoden (Switzerland)
description: A square white field charged with a black bear rampant (red claws and tongue), accompanied by the black letters 'V R' for Ussere Rhoden.
history: Appenzell split into Inner- and Ausserrhoden in 1597. The letters V (representing the /u/ sound) and R stand for Ussere Rhoden, the 'outer districts'; they are documented on flags from the 17th and 18th centuries.

features.role: bear rampant, armed and langued red
the letters 'V R' for Ussere Rhoden (outer districts)
